growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Artificial or natural seawater medium + NaNO2 + trace elements + cyanocobalamin
growth_records.cite: Bayer, Barbara et al. Carbon content, carbon fixation yield and dissolved organic carbon release from diverse marine nitrifiers. Limnology and Oceanography (2023). DOI:10.1002/lno.12252. [PMC10092583]
growth_records.method: not reported (cell counts by flow cytometry; cell numbers increased after NO2- depletion indicating partial decoupling)
growth_records.snippet: Nitrospina sp. Nb-3 ... Artificial seawater DIC fixation yield 0.032 ± 0.002; Natural seawater 0.043 ± 0.004 ... Biomass yield 0.056 ± 0.005 (ASW) / 0.076 ± 0.010 (NSW) gBio gN-1 ... ~1.4 times more efficient in natural vs artificial seawat
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (nitrite-oxidizing bacterium, Nitrospinae). Two media (ASW & NSW) in one record (Table 2, 1 umol/L NO2-, 15C). DIC yields & biomass yields MEASURED by 14C. NSW ~1.4x higher yield than ASW (reduced-N sparing hypothesis). Nitrite oxidation partly DECOUPLED from DIC fixation (cell counts rose after NO2- depletion; longer incubations gave 1.4-1.7x higher yields). C:N=3.41±0.05. No mu/absolute biomass-specific rate in main text.
